01_initial_request.input_data: Multilocus genetic sequences and morphological data (shell and anatomical traits) from various populations
01_initial_request.user_intent: To investigate the mode of morphological evolution (morphostatic radiation) and resolve taxonomic uncertainties within Radomaniola
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Brownian motion rate of morphological evolution and a time-calibrated species tree

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Radomaniola taxonomy is uncertain with 19 recognized species based mostly on shell morphology. [Gap] Specimens from across the distributional range are needed to assess diversity. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive sampling, chose to use Field collection to perform specimen gathering on 53 sites across the Balkan and Apennine Peninsulas, expecting to obtain preserved snail samples.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Specimens obtained from 53 sites, spanning the entire distributional range of Radomaniola except Turkey.

references.relevance: Provides distributional range information for sampling strategy.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Specimens collected and preserved in ethanol. [Gap] Genomic DNA is required for phylogenetic analysis. [Decision] To achieve DNA extraction, chose to use DNeasy Blood & Tissue kit / CTAB protocol to perform total genomic DNA extraction on 92 whole snails, expecting to obtain purified DNA.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Total genomic DNA extracted from 92 individuals.

references.relevance: Describes the CTAB protocol used for DNA extraction.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Genomic DNA extracted. [Gap] Specific gene fragments need amplification for sequencing. [Decision] To achieve gene amplification, chose to use PCR to perform amplification of COI, 16S, and 28S gene fragments using specific primer pairs, expecting to obtain amplified DNA products.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Gene fragments amplified for COI (658 bp), 16S (510 bp), and 28S (1052 bp).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PCR products obtained. [Gap] Nucleotide sequences need to be determined. [Decision] To achieve sequencing, chose to use ABI 3730 XL sequencer to perform sequencing using Big Dye Terminator kit v.3.1, expecting to obtain raw sequence data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Forward and reverse sequences generated for all gene fragments.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw sequences generated. [Gap] Sequences need assembly and editing to remove ambiguous ends. [Decision] To achieve sequence assembly, chose to use SEQUENCHER v.4.1.4 to perform assembly and editing of forward and reverse strands, expecting to obtain contiguous consensus sequences.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Resulting strands assembled and deposited in GenBank.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Consensus sequences obtained. [Gap] COI gene fragments need alignment for phylogenetic analysis. [Decision] To achieve alignment, chose to use MEGA v.7.0.14 to perform manual alignment of COI gene fragments, expecting to obtain aligned COI dataset.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: COI gene fragments aligned manually.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] COI aligned. [Gap] Ribosomal DNA fragments (16S and 28S) need alignment. [Decision] To achieve alignment, chose to use MAFFT v.7.402 to perform alignment with default settings for gap penalties, expecting to obtain aligned rDNA datasets.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ribosomal DNA fragments aligned.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Alignments complete. [Gap] Best-fitting models of nucleotide substitution need identification. [Decision] To achieve model selection, chose to use jModelTest v.2.1.7 to perform model testing using corrected Akaike information criterion (AICc), expecting to obtain preferred substitution models.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Preferred substitution models identified for each gene partition.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Substitution models selected. [Gap] Maximum likelihood phylogenetic trees need inference. [Decision] To achieve phylogeny inference, chose to use RAxML BlackBox webserver to perform ML analyses with ten random starting trees, expecting to obtain ML trees with branch supports.

parameters.support: rapid bootstrapping (BS) with an automatic cut-off

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ML trees inferred with branch support values (BS).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ML trees inferred. [Gap] Bayesian inference trees needed for comparison and support. [Decision] To achieve phylogeny inference, chose to use MrBayes v.3.2.7a to perform Bayesian inference through two independent runs of four MCMCs, expecting to obtain Bayesian trees with posterior probabilities.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Bayesian trees inferred with branch support values (BPPs).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phylogenetic trees available. [Gap] Species diversity needs assessment via distance-based method. [Decision] To achieve species delimitation, chose to use ABGD Web interface to perform automatic barcode gap discovery, expecting to obtain groups representing hypothetical species.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 28 putative species identified with a barcode gap of 0.9%.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] COI sequences available. [Gap] Ultrametric trees needed for bGMYC input. [Decision] To achieve tree inference for delimitation, chose to use BEAST v.1.8.4 to perform phylogeny inference calibrated in relative time, expecting to obtain 100 post-burn-in phylogenies.

parameters.calibration: relative time (rate of one substitution per site per unit of time)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 100 random post-burn-in phylogenies inferred.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ultrametric trees obtained. [Gap] Species delimitation via GMYC model needed. [Decision] To achieve species delimitation, chose to use bGMYC (R v.4.1.0) to perform Bayesian GMYC analysis, expecting to obtain species groups based on time threshold.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 30 groups retrieved as the most likely model.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phylogenies available. [Gap] Species delimitation via PTP model needed. [Decision] To achieve species delimitation, chose to use bPTP Webserver to perform Bayesian PTP analysis, expecting to obtain species groups based on substitutions.

parameters.input: RAxML tree pruned of identical haplotypes and outgroups

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 28 species suggested.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] bPTP results available. [Gap] Multi-rate PTP delimitation needed for accuracy. [Decision] To achieve species delimitation, chose to use mPTP v.0.1.1 to perform multi-rate PTP analysis, expecting to obtain species groups accommodating multiple thresholds.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 11 species suggested.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Taxonomic units identified by total evidence. [Gap] Time-calibrated species tree needed for trait evolution analysis. [Decision] To achieve species tree inference, chose to use *BEAST (BEAST v.1.8.4) to perform multispecies coalescent analysis with external substitution rate calibration, expecting to obtain a time-calibrated MCC tree.

parameters.substitution_rate: 0.81±0.24% substitutions per lineage per million years

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Time-calibrated species tree generated showing divergence ~5.5 Mya for Radomaniola s.s.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Species tree available. [Gap] Morphological data needed for trait evolution analysis. [Decision] To achieve data collection, chose to use Keyence VHX2000E digital microscope to perform measurements of 40 continuous traits on shells and soft bodies, expecting to obtain morphometric dataset.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Morphometric data collected for 40 traits across species.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Morphometric data collected. [Gap] Brownian motion rate needs estimation. [Decision] To achieve rate estimation, chose to use mvMORPH v.1.1.4 to perform BM model fitting with ML, expecting to obtain starting values for Bayesian analysis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ML estimates obtained for BM model parameters.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ML estimates available. [Gap] Bayesian inference of rates needed to incorporate uncertainty. [Decision] To achieve rate estimation, chose to use spBayes v.0.4.3 to perform MCMC sampling using ML estimates as starting values, expecting to obtain posterior distributions of rates.

parameters.priors: normal priors with mean equal to ML estimates

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Bayesian inference of rates and correlation completed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] BM rates estimated. [Gap] Mode of morphological evolution needs comparison across models. [Decision] To achieve mode comparison, chose to use mvMORPH v.1.1.4 to perform fitting of ten evolutionary models (BM, OU, EB, etc.) and compare via AICc, expecting to identify best-supported mode.

parameters.models: ten models (BM, OU, OUshift, EB, BMshift, Pkappa, WN, etc.)
parameters.trees: MCC tree and 100 post-burn-in posterior trees

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Shell and radula found to evolve toward a single optimum (OU); genitalia evolved rapidly (WN).

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Phylogenetic comparative methods and molecular species delimitation congruence

brownian_motion_rate.interpretation: Female genitalia evolved 18 times faster than nervous system; shell evolution constrained.

divergence_time.interpretation: Radomaniola s.s. clade started to diverge ~5.5 Mya (HPD 8.5-3.0 Mya).

species_count.interpretation: 21 species groups recognized through total evidence approach.

model_fit.interpretation: Shell and radula evolution best fit by Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model (single optimum).

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. A time-calibrated species tree was inferred, and the mode of morphological evolution was resolved, revealing morphostatic radiation for shell traits constrained by a single optimum, while anatomical traits diverged rapidly.
